items.title: Police arrest Proton Inspira driver after viral three-vehicle crash on NKVE
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Police have arrested a 26-year-old Proton Inspira driver following a three-vehicle collision on the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) that went viral on social media, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the accident occurred at about 11pm on May 9 at Km28 of the highway heading towards the Duta toll plaza. “The impact caused the vehicles to veer into the left road barrier, resulting in heavy damage to all three vehicles,” he said in a statement cited by FMT.

items.title: EPF bolsters retirement savings with new initiatives
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) has introduced new initiatives to strengthen retirement security across generations including i-Legasi, i-Emas and a Retirement Goal Calculator. EPF chief executive officer Ahmad Zulqarnain Onn said the initiatives reflect a shift in retirement planning from simply accumulating savings to ensuring long-term adequacy and sustainability. "As life expectancy rises, retirement planning must go beyond savings accumulation to focus on adequacy and sustainability.

items.title: ACE Market-bound MM Computer Systems aims to RM26.18mil from IPO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Information technology (IT) solutions provider MM Computer Systems Bhd (MMCS) aims to raise RM26.18 million from its initial public offering (IPO) via its listing on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd on June 11, 2026. The IPO comprises a public issue of 119 million new shares priced at 22 sen per share, alongside an offer for sale of 47.34 million existing shares. Managing director and chief executive officer Young Yoong Chang said enterprise IT environments are becoming increasingly complex and security-sensitive.

items.title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US–Iran peace talks falter
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday, as a lack of progress in US–Iran peace negotiations pushed oil prices higher, fuelling concerns that elevated inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer. Spot gold fell 0.8 per cent at US$4,676.02 per ounce, as of 0726 GMT. US gold futures for June delivery lost one per cent at US$4,684.50.

items.title: King graces photo session for Umno's 80th anniversary at Istana Besar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today graced a photography session at Tangga Selat, Istana Besar Johor, in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary celebration. According to a post on His Majesty's official Facebook page, accompanying the King was Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, the Regent of Johor. The royal arrival was received by Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

items.title: Plan to trim T20 fuel subsidies 'disheartening'; MP warns of ripple effect
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A government proposal to review fuel subsidies for the T20 income group has sparked concern from an opposition lawmaker. He said fuel subsidies should remain a shared national benefit rather than being segmented strictly by income class. "Fuel subsidies should be enjoyed by all Malaysians, regardless of income level.

items.title: Ukraine reports Russian attacks and battlefield clashes despite ceasefire
items.summary: KYIV: Ukraine's military and regional officials on Monday reported Russian drone strikes and battlefield clashes over the past 24 hours, despite a US-brokered ceasefire. In Ukraine, one person was killed and two people were wounded in the southeastern region of Zaporizhzhia, the regional governor said. Two were killed and two wounded in the southern region of Kherson, the Kherson region's governor said.

items.title: BSN names Mujibburrahman Abd Rashid acting CEO as Jay Khairil moves to KWAP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) has appointed its deputy chief executive Mujibburrahman Abd Rashid as acting chief executive following the departure of Datuk Jay Khairil. In a statement, BSN said that Mujibburrahman has served the bank since 2018 and brings extensive experience in banking operations, management and business. The bank also confirmed that Jay Khairil will transition from his role as BSN chief executive to become Kumpulan Wang Persaraan (Diperbadankan) (KWAP) chief executive officer, effective May 20.

items.title: 'I only have RM5,000': Man charged with robbing aunt pleads for lower bail
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A factory worker pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to robbing his aunt of a gold bracelet worth RM15,000 last month. Nor Anuar Jabar, 35, made a plea for lower bail, claiming he could only afford to pay RM5,000. Judge Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh set bail at RM10,000 and Anuar failed to post bail.

items.title: Penang DAP Kampung Kolam branch first to declare Anthony Loke welcome to contest in the state
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Kampung Kolam DAP branch has passed a resolution to welcome and support party secretary-general Anthony Loke to contest in Penang, marking the first time a branch in the state has openly made such a call. Ng said the resolution was made with the aspiration of leading Penang towards greater progress. He noted that while Penang has always been a frontline state for DAP, which has never abandoned its goal of making the state a political fortress, the current state leadership is increasingly plagued by issues such as uneven leadership quality, factional infighting, and money-driven political campaigns.

items.title: Driver arrested after three-vehicle NKVE crash video goes viral [WATCH]
items.summary: He said the crash involved a Toyota Hilux, a Proton Wira and a Proton Inspira. "A Proton Wira driven by a 40-year-old Indonesian man then crashed into the vehicle before a Toyota Hilux driven by a 41-year-old local man also collided with the Proton Inspira. He said the Toyota Hilux and Proton Wira sustained damage in the incident.

items.title: Three youths held over viral 'wheelie' stunt in Lipis
items.summary: LIPIS: Three youths believed to have performed dangerous "wheelie" stunts on their motorcycles in a video that went viral on Facebook were detained during a special operation here yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Ismail Man said the video, captioned "meriah bandar Lipis ini" (Lipis town is lively) and uploaded on May 5, showed motorcyclists performing wheelie stunts. "Acting on information, eight policemen conducted a special operation codenamed 'Op Samseng Jalanan' in Lipis town centre about 9pm yesterday and arrested three people.

items.title: Lorry driver charged with attempted murder in Bangsar shooting case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A lorry driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of attempting to murder a man during a shooting incident outside an entertainment centre in Bangsar last month. If the act causes injury, the accused may face up to 20 years' imprisonment. "Therefore, I object to the bail application and request the court not to grant bail," he said.

items.title: Bangsar shooting case: Lorry driver charged with attempted murder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — A lorry driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of attempting to murder a man during a shooting incident outside an entertainment centre in Bangsar last month. If the act causes injury, the accused may face up to 20 years’ imprisonment. Therefore, I object to the bail application and request the court not to grant bail,” he said.

items.title: US spending on operation against Iran tops US$77 billion
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The cost of the US military operation against Iran continues to grow and has already exceeded US$77 billion by the 71st day of the operation, according to the Iran War Cost Tracker portal, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. The calculation methodology is based on a Pentagon report to Congress, which stated that the first six days cost US$11.3 billion, with costs expected to reach US$1 billion per day thereafter. In late April, Under Secretary of Defence (Comptroller) and Chief Financial Officer Jules Hurst told lawmakers at a hearing of the House Armed Services Committee that US costs related to the conflict with Iran were approximately US$25 billion.

items.title: Six new senators sworn-in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 —Thirteen members of the Senate, including six new appointees, took their oath of office today before the Senate president, Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah. The six new appointees are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Wanita MCA chief Datuk Wong You Fong, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R.Thiagarajah. Meanwhile, six senators were reappointed, namely Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Datuk Seri Profesor Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyitm.

items.title: Philippine lawmakers to vote on impeachment of presidential hopeful Sara Duterte
items.summary: MANILA: Lawmakers in the Philippines are set to vote on Monday on whether to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, in what could create a major hurdle for her bid to run for the presidency in 2028. A house justice committee last month found probable cause for her impeachment in a petition from activists accusing her of misusing public funds, accumulating unexplained wealth and of threatening the lives of ⁠President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, his wife and the former house speaker. With ally-turned-enemy Marcos limited by the constitution to a single term in office, Duterte is the clear favourite to succeed him in 2028.

items.title: Turkish Airlines aircraft evacuated in Nepal after landing gear fire incident
items.summary: KATHMANDU: A Turkish Airlines plane was evacuated after smoke was observed from its landing gear while it was taxiing after landing in Nepal's capital of Kathmandu, the airline said, forcing the closure of the airport for an hour. All 277 passengers and 11 crew on board were evacuated using the emergency exits, and nobody was injured, the aviation regulator and airline said. Initial assessments indicate that the smoke was caused by a technical malfunction in a hydraulic pipe," Yahya Ustun, the airline's senior vice president, communications, said on social media platform X.

items.title: Penang opposition leader alleges Chief Minister Incorporated accounts not fully audited
items.summary: He said CMI had failed to present its complete and audited accounts. “The accounts were also not tabled in the state legislative assembly even though CMI manages the state revenue and multi-million ringgit projects,” he said when debating the Penang Yang DiPertua Negri’s speech at the state legislative assembly today. The Sungai Dua assemblyman questioned the transparency of the state’s revenue management under CMI.

items.title: Mixue expands cold chain strategy amid rising F&B competition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Food & beverage (F&B) companies such as Mixue Malaysia are beefing up their expansion and product quality strategy through a major cold chain upgrade initiative as competition intensifies in the country's fast-growing affordable premium beverage segment. The company has begun gradually rolling out cold chain-enhanced products under its broader 2026 quality upgrade strategy, positioning Malaysia as one of its key Southeast Asian growth markets amid evolving consumer expectations and rising industry competition. "We are upgrading our fruit ingredients from room-temperature storage to a full cold chain system.

items.title: EU expected to agree on long-stalled sanctions on Israeli settlers: Kallas
items.summary: BRUSSELS: The European Union was expected Monday to agree new sanctions on Israeli settlers over violence against Palestinians, the bloc's top diplomat said, as a change of government in Hungary ends months of blockage. "I expect political agreement on the sanctions on violent settlers, hopefully we will get there," EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said ahead of a meeting of the bloc's foreign ministers. EU officials said seven settlers or settler organisations were set to be blacklisted.

items.title: Sg Juru pollution crisis threatens fishermen, shellfish farmers
items.summary: JURU: About 100 shellfish farmers and fishermen in Kampung Kuala Juru here are growing increasingly alarmed over worsening pollution in Sungai Juru, which they say is destroying catches and threatening livelihoods built over generations. Kuala Juru Fishermen Association chairman Wahid Othman, 50, said the crisis had worsened over the past year, particularly after a waste discharge gate at Sungai Derhaka, which connects to Sungai Juru, was reportedly reopened after being closed for some time. CAP president Mohideen Abdul Kader said urgent measures should include closing all suspected discharge points along Sungai Juru to prevent further pollution.

items.title: RHB seals RM1bil financing deal for SMEs and mid-tier companies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Banking Group has inked a strategic partnership with Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Bhd (SJPP), a wholly‑owned entity of the Minister of Finance Inc, to provide up to RM1 billion in financing for small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and mid‑tier companies (MTCs). In a statement, RHB said the initiative is part of broader efforts to support businesses facing challenges stemming from the ongoing Middle East conflict, while also strengthening access to funding for viable companies. Datuk Mohd Rashid Mohamad, group managing director/group chief executive officer of RHB Banking Group, said, "Through our partnership with SJPP and participation in SME SRF, we are committed to helping viable SMEs and MTCs access financing and navigate current challenges with greater resilience."

items.title: Old car plate numbers win Selangor man RM14.8 million jackpot
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 52-year-old man won the Toto 4D Jackpot 1 worth RM14.8 million from the total RM16.8 million jackpot on May 6 by betting on his old car registration numbers. The property manager from Selangor bet on the same pair of winning numbers - 9186 and 4828 - derived from the registration numbers of his old cars, which he said carried "sentimental value." "I never expected both numbers would strike in the same draw," he said.

items.title: Penang tightens entry-point health security despite low hantavirus risk in Malaysia
items.summary: State exco for health, youth and sports Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said steps have been taken to tighten international entry screening measures. However the state health department has continued to implement health security protocols. “We have health officers stationed at international entry points including at the ports,” he said.

items.title: Man charged with elderly neighbour's murder in Taman Desa Aman
items.summary: IPOH: An unemployed man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with the murder of his elderly neighbour last week. Foon Choon Keong, 53, nodded after the charge was read before Magistrate Evangelin Simon Silvaraj, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. According to the charge, Foon, who is unmarried, is accused of murdering Woo Chui Fun , 70, at a house in Persiaran Pegoh Aman 2, Taman Desa Aman, between 4.40pm and 10pm on May 4, 2026.

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak renews painful memories for Patagonian village
items.summary: MAILEN Valle lost her father and two sisters during a hantavirus outbreak more than seven years ago in Epuyen, a village in Argentina's Patagonia region. With the recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship, hard memories have resurfaced for the 33-year-old. And at that table there were several people who got infected, and people died," Mailen recalled.

items.title: Bank Negara, Bank Indonesia sign MoU to strengthen bilateral cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia and Bank Indonesia (BI) have signed a new memorandum of understanding (MoU) that strengthens bilateral cooperation between the two central banks. In a joint statement today, they said that the MoU provides a comprehensive platform to guide cooperation in a range of central banking areas. Bank Negara governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said the MoU reaffirms the longstanding partnership between Bank Negara and BI.

items.title: Ah longs hurl Molotov cocktails at JB homes, four families living in fear
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Four families in Bukit Indah are living in fear after their homes along Jalan Indah were hit by Molotov cocktail attacks over two consecutive days. Perling assemblyman Liew Chin Tong said the attacks, which occurred last Friday and Saturday, are believed to be the work of an unlicensed moneylending syndicate based in Singapore. He said the petrol bombs were targeted at one house, but the fire spread to three neighbouring properties.

items.title: Nepalese worker jailed three months for breaking colleague's arm with iron rod
items.summary: Bikash Giri, 30, pleaded guilty before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass to a charge under Section 325 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt. Giri had struck the victim on the right arm and the back of the head with an iron rod. The assault left the victim with a broken right arm, while Giri required five stitches to his head following the altercation.

items.title: Cambodian man missing after immigration raid found drowned in Sungai Pahang
items.summary: PEKAN: A Cambodian man who went missing after his wife and six children were detained in an Immigration Department raid was found drowned in Sungai Pahang here yesterday. Pekan district police chief Superintendent Mohd Zaidi Mat Zin said the victim's younger brother lodged a police report about 5pm on May 9 after failing to locate him. Zaidi said checks with the Pahang Immigration Department found only the victim's wife and six children were in custody following the raid, while the victim was missing.

items.title: Malaysia's volume index of services up by 5.8pct in Q1 FY26: Statistics Department
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's volume index of services recorded a year-on-year growth of 5.8 per cent in the first quarter (1Q) of 2026, reaching 168.0 points, according to the Statistics Department. "Meanwhile, the business services and finance segment grew by 3.0 per cent, while the other services segment registered a higher growth of 9.3 per cent," he said. The department said that the volume index for the wholesale and retail trade, food and beverage and accommodation segment rose by 5.7 per cent to 166.3 points, supported by growth in wholesale and retail trade (4.9 per cent), food and beverage (8.5 per cent) and accommodation (15.0 per cent).

items.title: Family proud Singaporean hiker died trying to save others in Mount Dukono eruption
items.summary: GALELA: The family of Singaporean hiker Timothy Heng, who died during the Mount Dukono volcano eruption in North Halmahera, Indonesia, said they were proud he spent his final moments trying to help others. According to The Straits Times, the 30-year-old was among two Singaporeans killed after the active volcano erupted on May 8, sending hot rocks and ash raining down on a group of hikers near the summit. She said her parents and Heng's soon-to-be fiancée were especially devastated as they had hoped he would be found alive.

items.title: Gamuda unit begins work on Australia's Jinbi solar farm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gamuda Bhd's Australian subsidiary, DT Infrastructure, has begun work on the Jinbi Solar Farm for Yindjibarndi Energy Corpn (YEC), a flagship 75-megawatt renewable energy project in Western Australia's Pilbara region. In a statement, DT Infrastructure chief executive John Anderson said the project marks an important step for both the company's growing renewable energy portfolio and the broader energy transition in Western Australia. "Partnering with one of Australia's largest Indigenous-led renewable energy initiatives allows us to support YEC in delivering meaningful, long-term outcomes for the Yindjibarndi community," he said.

items.title: MIDF approves Malaysia's first IP-backed financing facility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF) has approved its first financing facility under the World Intellectual Property Organisation–MIDF Intellectual Property (IP)-Backed Financing Pilot Programme. The first recipient, a Malaysian small and medium enterprise, obtained the facility by using its intellectual property assets as a key part of the credit assessment and security package. MIDF, a unit of MBSB Bhd, said the milestone marks a significant shift in Malaysia's financial landscape, with intellectual property now recognised as a form of collateral and security interest.

items.title: Sabah revises tourism strategy after airline route suspensions affect Kota Kinabalu connectivity
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Sabah is reviewing its tourism targets and adopting a more flexible strategy following a series of airline route suspensions affecting connectivity into the state capital. “We are already seeing these changes impacting airline capacity, with route suspensions and reduced frequencies affecting connectivity into Kota Kinabalu, and we are closely monitoring developments to ensure our tourism planning remains responsive,” he said in a statement. Among the affected routes are AirAsia’s temporary suspension of its Singapore–Kota Kinabalu route from May 13 to November 30, Jakarta–Kota Kinabalu from April 16 to May 21, and Ho Chi Minh–Kota Kinabalu beginning July 1.

items.title: 13 senators sworn in: Six new appointees, seven reappointed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirteen members of the Senate, including six new appointees, took their oath of office today before the Senate president, Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah. The six new appointees are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Wanita MCA chief Datuk Wong You Fong, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R.Thiagarajah. Meanwhile, six senators were reappointed, namely Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Datuk Seri Profesor Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyitm.

items.title: Wood cutter remanded for suspected abuse of five-year-old stepson
items.summary: Acting Dungun police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the 36-year-old man was arrested yesterday and brought to the Magistrate's Court here today for a remand order. He said the arrest followed a police report lodged by the victim's 30-year-old biological mother at the Bukit Besi police station at 5.36 pm yesterday. Following that, Jumaidy said the victim, who attends a kindergarten in Bukit Besi, was taken to Dungun Hospital for examination at 8 pm yesterday.

items.title: Hit-and-run at Borneo Marathon leaves runner, Rela personnel injured; organisers say police probing incident
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Organisers of the Borneo International Marathon 2026 have confirmed a hit-and-run incident during the race, which left a 40-year-old participant and a Rela personnel injured. The organisers also reminded motorists to respect road closures and safety measures during organised sporting events. We urge all road users to respect road closures, marathon safety measures, and the presence of runners and crews during organised sporting events,” they said.

items.title: Customs seizes illicit cash, drugs and liquor at KLIA
items.summary: SEPANG, May 11 — Customs officers at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) have foiled attempts to smuggle out cash and drugs, and seized smuggled liquor, with a total value of nearly RM1.12 million in four separate cases last month. KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said that in the first case on April 2, the department, in collaboration with the Aviation Security (AVSEC) unit of Malaysia Airports, detained an Indonesian traveller at the Satellite Building, Terminal 1, for allegedly attempting to take RM129,500 in cash out of the country without declaration. “The case is being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 and Section 74(1)(d) of the Excise Act 1976,” he said.

items.title: Penang urged to clarify REE policy amid concerns over defence-linked use and InvestPenang governance
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government has been urged to take a clear stance to ensure that rare earth elements (REE) from the state are not used for weapons-related purposes. He said that last month, 57 activist groups in Malaysia raised concerns over the alleged supply of REE to the United States Department of Defense (Pentagon). He added that environmental activists and anti-war groups had also raised concerns that REE from Malaysia could be used for weapons-related purposes.

items.title: Two supervisors killed after being flung from car in Second Link five-vehicle crash
items.summary: Iskandar Puteri police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the accident occurred at about 5.40am on the highway towards Senai after the victim’s car skidded and lost control. “The victim’s car then hit a road divider before causing a motorcycle to lose control and crash into the rear,” he said in a statement today. “The car’s second victim, a 34-year-old man, was thrown out of the car due to the impact and fell under the overhead bridge,” he said, adding that the victim was also pronounced dead at the scene.

items.title: Penang CM: Rising costs will not delay Penang’s strategic water projects
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government will continue with its water supply projects under the Water Contingency Plan 2030 amid rising operational and construction costs due to global economic uncertainties. Penang chief minister Chow Kon Yeow said the projects implemented by the Perbadanan Bekalan Air Pulau Pinang (PBAPP) are crucial to ensuring the stability of the state’s water supply. Chow also said the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) is postponing several projects for at least six months.

items.title: Penang sees surge in medical tourists, revenue crosses RM1.1b in 2025
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — Penang recorded strong growth in its medical tourism sector, with foreign patient numbers rising to 527,176 in 2025. State executive councillor for tourism and creative economy Wong Hon Wai said this represents a 25.94 per cent increase compared to the previous year. He said data from the Penang Centre of Medical Tourism (PMED), covering 16 private hospitals in the state, showed foreign patient numbers stood at 418,608 in 2024.

items.title: Nearly 4m-long crocodile caught by Sibu licensed team, one more croc escapes
items.summary: SIBU, May 11 — A Sibu licensed crocodile catching team captured a male crocodile measuring around 13 feet (3.96 metres) in length and weighing 280kg in the waters of Kampung Sebenak, Daro on Saturday. Team leader Jeferry Siew said the operation followed frequent sightings of crocodiles in the area, raising concern among residents. He said they had initially planned a two-week operation but within days, two crocodiles were caught in traps.

items.title: Putrajaya weighs RON95 subsidy cuts, admits tough call on who should lose aid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 11 — The government has acknowledged the difficulty in determining which Malaysians should be excluded from RON95 fuel subsidies as it fine-tunes its targeted subsidy mechanism. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said one of the key challenges was setting a clear cut-off point for eligibility without affecting groups that still require assistance. He said the government is now studying implementation details, including where the threshold for eligibility should be set.

items.title: George Town gazettes 35 heritage items, eyes Unesco recognition for Penang traditions
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government has gazetted seven cultural heritage arts items and 28 traditional food items this year under the Penang State Heritage Enactment 2011. Wong said all intangible cultural heritage items gazetted at the state level may also be recommended for national recognition under the National Heritage Act 2005. In January, the state government announced the gazettement of 10 traditional foods and six intangible heritage items as state heritage.

items.title: Putrajaya names three Malaysian firms to spearhead Made-By-Malaysia chip project
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's administration today handed offers to three local firms picked to spearhead the ambitious made-in-Malaysia chip design project, hailing it as a significant step in strengthening the nation’s capabilities in integrated circuit design. Four tokens, including Arm CSS (Compute Subsystems) and Arm Flexible Access (AFA), were granted to Great Asic Technology Sdn. Akmal said the offer opens access to Arm’s computing platforms and intellectual property (IP) portfolio to accelerate the development of local semiconductor products designed in Malaysia.

items.title: Ex-aide to Teresa Kok claims trial to graft, cheating charges linked to school funding applications
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — A former aide to Seputeh MP Teresa Kok pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to two charges involving cheating and allegedly receiving RM500,000 in gratification linked to school smart board applications, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). FMT reported that Edmund Teoh, 40, was accused of deceiving Kok into believing that only two schools in her constituency had applied for funding for interactive smart boards, when in fact 16 schools had submitted applications. Teoh also faced a charge of allegedly receiving RM500,000 from one Ainuddin Aminuddin, 36, as an inducement to help secure smart board supply contracts for 16 schools in Seputeh through TMTIT Solutions, a company owned by Ainuddin’s wife.

items.title: Penang has no plans to extend chief minister term limit beyond two terms, says Chow Kon Yeow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government currently has no plans to amend the chief minister’s two-term limit to 10 years, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. “In situations where the state legislative assembly is dissolved before a term reaches five years, the term ‘two terms’ should be understood as a cumulative calculation of the years involved in the first and second terms,” he said during the state assembly sitting today. Penang became the first state in Malaysia to formally legislate a two-term limit for the chief minister’s tenure.

items.title: Malaysian haj pilgrims reminded to follow miqat rules, heed Saudi regulations
items.summary: Miqat is a designated boundary or point where Muslims intending to perform haj or umrah (minor haj) must enter ihram before proceeding to Makkah. Tabung Haji Haj Expert Dr Mohd Sirajuddin Siswadi Putera Mohamed Shith said the issue mainly affects haj pilgrims performing Tamattu’ pilgrimage (a form of haj in which pilgrims perform umrah first, exit the state of ihram, and later re-enter ihram to perform the haj rituals). Mohd Sirajuddin Siswadi Putera said the rule does not affect pilgrims performing Haj Ifrad (performing haj first before umrah) and Haj Qiran (combining haj and umrah in one ihram), as they remain in ihram throughout the entire period.

items.title: Cops nab six men, bust cable theft syndicate in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 11 — Police have successfully crippled a cable theft syndicate following the arrest of six men in a series of operations around the Klang Valley from May 4 until yesterday. Shah Alam district police chief ACP Ramsay Embol said the operation was carried out by the Criminal Investigation Division (D4) of the Shah Alam District Police Headquarters (IPD) with the cooperation of various units after an incident involving the theft of Telekom Malaysia (TM) cables in Section 2 here went viral on social media platforms. Anyone with information related to the case is urged to contact Shah Alam IPD criminal investigating officer Insp Mohammad Amirul Zulkifli at 012-5583436 to assist investigations.

items.title: Four nabbed after long-running oil palm fruit thefts in Kanchong Darat uncovered
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT, May 11 — Police have arrested four local men suspected of stealing oil palm fruits in Kanchong Darat here after their activities were allegedly caught on closed-circuit television (CCTV), according to Sinar Harian. Kuala Langat police chief Supt Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi said police received a report at about 6.30pm on Friday from a local man regarding two suspicious vehicles seen in an oil palm plantation area. “Checks on CCTV footage found four suspects allegedly stealing oil palm fruits and loading them into the vehicles involved.

items.title: PM announces govt raising Mitra allocation to RM150m
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The government has agreed to increase the allocation to the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) from RM100 million previously to RM150 million, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said this was following the agency’s good performance in implementing Indian community development programmes. The Prime Minister said the decision was made after the government assessed the effectiveness of various Mitra programmes targeted at Indian community groups who need assistance, particularly in the fields of education, training and socio-economic development.

items.title: After last night’s heavy rains, 43 flood victims evacuated to two Perak relief centres
items.summary: IPOH, May 11 — A total of 43 people from 11 families have been evacuated to two relief centres (PPS) in two districts in Perak -- Larut, Matang and Selama, and Batang Padang—following floods that occurred last night. According to a statement by the District Disaster Management Committee Secretariat, the PPS at Sekolah Kebangsaan Matang Gelugor was opened at 1.30 am and is currently sheltering 37 people from nine families from Kampung Matang Pasir in the Larut, Matang and Selama district. Meanwhile, the PPS at Dewan Serbaguna Kampung Klah Baru in the Batang Padang district was opened at 10.30 pm, housing six victims from two families affected by floods in Kampung Klah Baru.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim departs Moscow after special visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has departed for home after concluding a special visit to Moscow. According to a post on the King’s official Facebook page, His Majesty’s private aircraft departed from Vnukovo-2 International Airport at 3.30pm local time yesterday (8.30pm Malaysian time). The special invitation from Russian President Vladimir Putin reflected the Russian government’s highest recognition of Malaysia.

items.title: Khairy Jamaluddin: Prime minister’s post still ‘lifetime ambition’, yet no longer the obsession it once was
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Khairy Jamaluddin still hopes to become Malaysia’s prime minister one day but stressed that he is no longer obsessed with holding the top post anymore. Khairy, a three-time Cabinet minister and former Umno youth chief, said becoming Malaysia’s prime minister has always been his “lifelong ambition” and that he used to consider not fulfilling that dream would make him a failure in life. “I always felt that I would not be a success in life if I didn’t become the prime minister,” Khairy said in an exclusive interview with Malay Mail recently.

items.title: Khairy admits he thought ‘long and hard’ about new party, but says Umno remains his ‘natural home’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — After his expulsion from Umno, Khairy Jamaluddin said he thought “long and hard” about setting up his own political party but chose not to do so for the sake of Malay unity. Khairy said launching his own party would have further fragmented the Malay voter base that was already in a four-way split involving Umno, PAS, Amanah and Pejuang. But Khairy said Umno was already present as his “natural home”.

items.title: Rising costs elsewhere, but this Perak stall in Selama keeps nasi lemak at 50 sen
items.summary: The stall owner credits the nasi lemak supplier Othman Che Mat for making it possible to keep the price at 50 sen, and enabling villagers to continue enjoying their favourite breakfast meal at an affordable cost. Othman said when he first began supplying nasi lemak to Warung Lejen, it was sold to customers at 30 sen per packet. Patrons enjoying the 50 sen nasi lemak at Warung Lejen in Selama, Perak.

items.title: Khairy likens Umno to a centrist party that can ‘work things out so that nobody loses’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Although it largely champions Malay-Muslim causes, Khairy Jamaluddin said Umno naturally positions itself as a centrist party. That is why I can exist in the party, and so can Akmal and Asyraf,” he said, referring to Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh and party secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. As such, he said the “UMDAP” accusations levelled by the Opposition to portray Umno as subservient to DAP in the unity government is completely baseless.
